articles.title: Magnitude 5.2 earthquake strikes eastern Türkiye, no damage reported
articles.description:
A magnitude-5.2 earthquake shook Türkiye’s eastern province of Van on Saturday, the Disaster and Emergency Management Presidency (AFAD) said.

There were no immediate reports of ca...

articles.title: Partnerships & Stakeholders: Science and Technology Community
articles.description: The role of global science, academia, research and innovation is enshrined in the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction where it is recognized for the catalytic, boundary-pushing and transformative potential it holds for addressing the dynamic drivers of disaster risk.

articles.title: Intra Africa, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) Disaster Risk Reduction Programme (DRRP)
articles.description: The Intra-ACP Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Programme represents one of the European Union’s key cooperation instruments with the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States (OACPS) in strengthening risk management and resilience to disasters.

articles.title: Online Workshop on Strengthening Whole-of-Society Capacities for Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Resilience: Inclusive Governance and Innovative Mechanisms
articles.description: The workshop will introduce concepts, frameworks, practical approaches to strengthen inclusive DRR, climate resilience, and climate action through effective governance, risk-informed planning, private sector engagement, and technology-enabled solutions.

articles.title: Concept note: The International day in memory of the victims of earthquakes
articles.description: The international day provides an annual opportunity for remembrance and renewed commitment to protecting lives and livelihoods from earthquake disasters.

articles.title: Why resilient infrastructure can no longer be optional
articles.description: In his three decades of responding to catastrophic events around the world, Kamal Kishore, head of the United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction, often observed a curious human tendency to overlook the invisible foundations of our safety.

articles.title: Advancing Urban Resilience in Uganda
articles.description: In February 2026, UNDRR convened Ugandan stakeholders at both the local and national levels to strengthen disaster resilience and climate change adaptation through the Making Cities Resilient 2030 (MCR2030) initiative.

articles.title: Study on the reach and effectiveness of Early Warning Systems in Timor-Leste
articles.description: This study evaluates the effectiveness of early warning systems in Timor-Leste, based on household surveys and focus group discussions across eight municipalities and key informant interviews with national, municipal and community stakeholders.

articles.title: Strengthening the disaster sesilience of MSMEs in Barbados
articles.description: Strengthening the Disaster Resilience of MSMEs in Barbados

articles.title: The Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea (RBKC)
articles.description: The Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea plays a central role in disaster risk reduction (DRR) at the local level by focusing on risk assessment and preparedness in coordination with wider London and national systems.

articles.title: Learn, Connect, Share: Exploring resources for students in disaster resilience
articles.description: Are you a student passionate about building a safer, more resilient world? Join us for a live introduction to PreventionWeb, UNDRR’s global platform for disaster risk reduction (DRR) knowledge.

articles.title: Greece strengthens urban resilience and expands national and local engagement with MCR2030
articles.description: UNDRR advanced partnerships and local resilience action in Greece through multiple meetings and events in Athens, reinforcing collaboration on urban resilience, extreme heat, wildfire risk governance, and community engagement.
